apply: Students will be able to use the equation v = λf to calculate the speed of a wave.
Students will be able to use the principle of superposition to find the resultant displacement of two or more waves.
Students will be able to use the concept of reflection to find the angle of incidence and angle of reflection.
Students will be able to use the equation y = A sin (kx - ωt) to describe the displacement of a progressive wave.
analyze: Students will be able to compare the characteristics of longitudinal and transverse waves.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the speed of a wave and the properties of the medium.
Students will be able to analyze the effect of superposition of waves on the resultant displacement.
Students will be able to analyze the effect of reflection of waves on the angle of incidence and angle of reflection.
remember: Students will be able to recall the types of waves (mechanical and electromagnetic) and their characteristics.
Students will be able to list the different modes of vibration of a wave (longitudinal and transverse).
Students will be able to identify the terms - frequency, wavelength and speed of a wave.
Students will be able to recall the relationship between speed, frequency and wavelength of a wave (v = λf).
understand: Students will be able to explain the formation of stationary waves and progressive waves.
Students will be able to describe the properties of stationary waves (nodes and antinodes).
Students will be able to explain the concept of superposition of waves.
Students will be able to describe the principle of reflection of waves.

ncert_objectives: To study the characteristics of waves and their types.
To understand the formation of stationary and progressive waves.
To study the properties of waves and their behavior on reflection.
To apply the concepts of superposition and interference to find the resultant displacement of waves.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the types of waves and their characteristics (Short Answer Questions).
To explain the formation of stationary and progressive waves (Long Answer Questions).
To describe the properties of waves and their behavior on reflection (Long Answer Questions).
To use the equation v = λf to calculate the speed of a wave (Numerical Value Questions).
